{{Quote|Known death stick dealer. May be armed.|Reez Andor's bounty brief|<ref name="Hunter" />}}
'''Reez Andor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] criminal.<ref name="Hunter" /> By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="date">The events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' take place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Andor was wanted dead or alive by [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police]] for being a known [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] [[Drug dealer|dealer]].<ref name="Hunter" /> His<ref name="HunterGuide" /> [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] noted his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was unknown but that his last known location was the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], offering a sum of 2,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] if he was captured alive or 1,250 if captured [[Death/Legends|dead]]. It also stated that he was affiliated with fellow death stick dealer [[Jervis Gloom]] and that he may be armed.<ref name="Hunter" /> Andor wore a [[Color/Legends|grey]] [[Eye/Legends|eye]] piece and [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] of varied shades of grey. His shoes were white and his [[Belt/Legends|belt]] had a pink oval in the center. Andor was bald and had tan skin with brown facial [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="Hunter" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
While the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] was hunting for Gloom on Coruscant, Fett encountered Andor<ref name="Hunter" /> waiting for an [[Air taxi/Legends|air taxi]]<ref name="HunterGuide" /> in the Entertainment District. Fett captured Andor alive and collected the 2,500 credits.<ref name="Hunter" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Reez Andor appeared in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' developed by [[LucasArts]].<ref name="Hunter" /> In the game, Andor is the tenth optional secondary bounty that the player can capture in the Coruscant Entertainment District and is labeled as the thirty-fifth bounty overall. Andor is able to be captured by the player for a higher sum of credits or killed for a lower amount.<ref name="Hunter" /> This article assumes that Jango Fett captured Andor alive for the full sum of credits.
